1.arabic: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم يا أيها المزمل
1.english: O you who wraps himself [in clothing],
1.transliteration: Yaw ayyuhal muzzammil

1.arabic: قم الليل إلا قليلا
1.english: Arise [to pray] the night, except for a little -
1.transliteration: Qumil laila illaa qaleelaa

1.arabic: نصفه أو انقص منه قليلا
1.english: Half of it - or subtract from it a little
1.transliteration: Nisfahooo awinqus minhu qaleelaa

1.arabic: أو زد عليه ورتل القرآن ترتيلا
1.english: Or add to it, and recite the Qur'an with measured recitation.
1.transliteration: Aw zid 'alaihi wa rattilil Qur'aana tarteela

1.arabic: إنا سنلقي عليك قولا ثقيلا
1.english: Indeed, We will cast upon you a heavy word.
1.transliteration: Innaa sanulqee 'alika qawalan saqeelaa

1.arabic: إن ناشئة الليل هي أشد وطئا وأقوم قيلا
1.english: Indeed, the hours of the night are more effective for concurrence [of heart and tongue] and more suitable for words.
1.transliteration: Inn naashi'atal laili hiya ashadddu wat anw wa aqwamu qeelaa

1.arabic: إن لك في النهار سبحا طويلا
1.english: Indeed, for you by day is prolonged occupation.
1.transliteration: Inna laka fin nahaari sabhan taweelaa

1.arabic: واذكر اسم ربك وتبتل إليه تبتيلا
1.english: And remember the name of your Lord and devote yourself to Him with [complete] devotion.
1.transliteration: Wazkuris ma rabbika wa tabattal ilaihi tabteelaa

1.arabic: رب المشرق والمغرب لا إله إلا هو فاتخذه وكيلا
1.english: [He is] the Lord of the East and the West; there is no deity except Him, so take Him as Disposer of [your] affairs.
1.transliteration: Rabbul mashriqi wal maghriibi laaa ilaaha illaa Huwa fattakhizhu wakeelaa

1.arabic: واصبر على ما يقولون واهجرهم هجرا جميلا
1.english: And be patient over what they say and avoid them with gracious avoidance.
1.transliteration: Wasbir 'alaa maa yaqoo loona wahjurhum hajran jameelaa

1.arabic: وذرني والمكذبين أولي النعمة ومهلهم قليلا
1.english: And leave Me with [the matter of] the deniers, those of ease [in life], and allow them respite a little.
1.transliteration: Wa zarnee walmukaz zibeena ulin na'mati wa mahhilhum qaleelaa

1.arabic: إن لدينا أنكالا وجحيما
1.english: Indeed, with Us [for them] are shackles and burning fire
1.transliteration: Inna ladainaaa ankaalanw wa jaheemaa

1.arabic: وطعاما ذا غصة وعذابا أليما
1.english: And food that chokes and a painful punishment -
1.transliteration: Wa ta'aaman zaa ghussa tinw wa'azaaban aleemaa

1.arabic: يوم ترجف الأرض والجبال وكانت الجبال كثيبا مهيلا
1.english: On the Day the earth and the mountains will convulse and the mountains will become a heap of sand pouring down.
1.transliteration: Yawma tarjuful ardu waljibaalu wa kaanatil jibaalu kaseebam maheelaa

1.arabic: إنا أرسلنا إليكم رسولا شاهدا عليكم كما أرسلنا إلى فرعون رسولا
1.english: Indeed, We have sent to you a Messenger as a witness upon you just as We sent to Pharaoh a messenger.
1.transliteration: Innaa arsalnaaa ilaikum rasoolan shahidan 'alikum kamaaa arsalnaaa ilaa Fir'awna Rasoolna

1.arabic: فعصى فرعون الرسول فأخذناه أخذا وبيلا
1.english: But Pharaoh disobeyed the messenger, so We seized him with a ruinous seizure.
1.transliteration: Fa'asaa Fir'awnur Rasoola fa akhaznaahu akhzanw wabeelaa

1.arabic: فكيف تتقون إن كفرتم يوما يجعل الولدان شيبا
1.english: Then how can you fear, if you disbelieve, a Day that will make the children white- haired?
1.transliteration: Fakaifa tattaqoona in kafartum yawmany yaj'alul wildaana sheeba

1.arabic: السماء منفطر به ۚ كان وعده مفعولا
1.english: The heaven will break apart therefrom; ever is His promise fulfilled.
1.transliteration: Assamaaa'u munfatirum bih; kaana wa'duhoo maf'oola

1.arabic: إن هذه تذكرة ۖ فمن شاء اتخذ إلى ربه سبيلا
1.english: Indeed, this is a reminder, so whoever wills may take to his Lord a way.
1.transliteration: Inna haazihee tazkiratun fa man shaaa'at takhaza ilaa Rabbihee sabeelaa

1.arabic: ۞ إن ربك يعلم أنك تقوم أدنى من ثلثي الليل ونصفه وثلثه وطائفة من الذين معك ۚ والله يقدر الليل والنهار ۚ علم أن لن تحصوه فتاب عليكم ۖ فاقرءوا ما تيسر من القرآن ۚ علم أن سيكون منكم مرضى ۙ وآخرون يضربون في الأرض يبتغون من فضل الله ۙ وآخرون يقاتلون في سبيل الله ۖ فاقرءوا ما تيسر منه ۚ وأقيموا الصلاة وآتوا الزكاة وأقرضوا الله قرضا حسنا ۚ وما تقدموا لأنفسكم من خير تجدوه عند الله هو خيرا وأعظم أجرا ۚ واستغفروا الله ۖ إن الله غفور رحيم
1.english: Indeed, your Lord knows, [O Muhammad], that you stand [in prayer] almost two thirds of the night or half of it or a third of it, and [so do] a group of those with you. And Allah determines [the extent of] the night and the day. He has known that you [Muslims] will not be able to do it and has turned to you in forgiveness, so recite what is easy [for you] of the Qur'an. He has known that there will be among you those who are ill and others traveling throughout the land seeking [something] of the bounty of Allah and others fighting for the cause of Allah. So recite what is easy from it and establish prayer and give zakah and loan Allah a goodly loan. And whatever good you put forward for yourselves - you will find it with Allah. It is better and greater in reward. And seek forgiveness of Allah. Indeed, Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.
1.transliteration: Inna Rabbaka ya'lamu annaka taqoomu adnaa min sulusa yil laili wa nisfahoo wa sulusahoo wa taaa'ifatum minal lazeena ma'ak; wal laahu yuqaddirul laila wanna haar; 'alima al lan tuhsoohu fataaba 'alaikum faqra'oo maa tayassara minal quraan; 'alima an sa yakoonu minkum mardaa wa aakharoona yadriboona fil ardi yabtaghoona min fadlil laahi wa aakharoona yuqaatiloona fee sabeelil laahi faqra'oo ma tayassara minhu wa aqeemus salaata wa aatuz zakaata wa aqridul laaha qardan hasanaa; wa maa tuqadimoo li anfusikum min khairin tajidoohu 'indal laahi huwa khayranw wa a'zama ajraa; wastaghfirul laahaa innal laaha ghafoorur raheem.
